projects.address: Mayfield Rd & McLaughlin Rd, Caledon, ON

hero.headline: Caledon Trails: 20-Foot Towns & Detached Homes
hero.subhead: Master-planned community by Laurier Homes and Yorkwood Homes at Mayfield Road and McLaughlin Road in fast-growing South Caledon.

page_plan.story_md:
Caledon Trails is a master-planned community by Laurier Homes and Yorkwood Homes featuring towns, semis and single-detached homes at the intersection of Mayfield Road and McLaughlin Road in South Caledon. The community sits on the Brampton–Caledon border, blending small-town Caledon character with quick access to the GTA's highway and GO network. With 1,200 planned units, this is one of the area's most significant new releases.

The townhome program is built on 20-foot lots and runs from the 1,714 sq ft Holly to the 2,580 sq ft Hickory end unit — every floorplan offers a double-car garage and three bedrooms (with several plans configurable up to five or six bedrooms plus optional in-law or bachelor suites). Detached releases include 30', 36' and 42' frontages reaching up to roughly 3,500 sq ft, with pricing on the detached collection starting from $1.1 Million.

This is a deliberate investor and end-user play. Caledon's population is forecast to climb from 80,000 to over 200,000 by 2041, Highway 410 provides access to the 407, 401 and 403, and Peel Region faces a projected shortfall of roughly 174,900 homes by 2031 — supply pressure that supports long-run pricing. The community sits about 5 minutes from Highway 410 and 10 minutes from Mount Pleasant GO Station, which offers GO Train, GO Bus and Zum service.

Incentives at the VIP launch include $7,500 capped development charges, a finished basement package valued up to $75,000 on select lots, a stainless appliance package, 9-foot second-floor ceilings, assignment rights, and the HST rebate included (up to $130,000 in savings, subject to eligibility).

selling_points.title: Rare 20-Foot Double-Garage Towns
selling_points.body: Six townhome designs from 1,714 to 2,580 sq ft, every plan with a double-car garage and three bedrooms expandable up to five or six with optional in-law or bachelor suites.

selling_points.title: Two Veteran GTA Builders
selling_points.body: Built by Laurier Homes (since 1976) and Yorkwood Homes (since the 1950s), whose combined legacy includes more than 10,000 homes and award-winning communities.

selling_points.title: Highway 410 & Mount Pleasant GO
selling_points.body: Five minutes from Highway 410, ten minutes from Mount Pleasant GO Station, and under an hour to Toronto's Union Station by GO Bus.

selling_points.body: $7,500 cap on development levies protects buyers from closing-cost escalation — a tangible savings on a pre-construction purchase.

selling_points.title: Finished Basement Up to $75K Value
selling_points.body: On select lots, the basement is delivered finished with a rec room, bathroom and wet bar — value up to $75,000 (not available on move-in-ready inventory).

selling_points.title: Caledon's Long-Term Growth Story
selling_points.body: Caledon's population is forecast to grow from 80,000 to over 200,000 by 2041, while Peel Region faces a projected shortfall of 174,900 homes by 2031.

hero.headline: Keswick Heights Townhomes — Southwest Edmonton
hero.subhead: Pre-construction 3-bed, 2.5-bath condo townhomes in Keswick, Edmonton's fastest-growing southwest community, starting from the low $330s.

page_plan.story_md:
Keswick Heights Townhomes is a pre-construction collection of modern condo townhouses by Miracle Team in the Keswick community of Southwest Edmonton — a master-planned neighbourhood bordered by the North Saskatchewan River and minutes from the Windermere retail corridor. Two floorplans are offered: Model T at 1,114 sqft from $334,900 and Model P at 1,302 sqft from $369,900, both with three bedrooms, 2.5 bathrooms, 9-foot ceilings and quartz countertops throughout.

For end-users, the appeal is space and finish at a southwest Edmonton entry price: Model P includes a double attached garage, a full unfinished basement to grow into, and a rear deck, while every home benefits from a low $190/month condo fee covering property insurance, common-area maintenance, grass cutting and snow removal. Keswick itself is built around walking trails, constructed wetlands, the Arbour of Keswick playground, Keswick Pond, and two area schools (Joan Carr and Dr. Anne Anderson High School).

For investors, the structure is straightforward and capital-efficient. The deposit is 5% on signing and 5% in six months — well under the 15–20% typically required on Ontario pre-construction — and the contract permits assignment for a flat $2,500 + GST, providing a clean exit before closing. A $6,500 appliance credit at The Brick Store further reduces move-in cost. Edmonton's relative affordability versus Toronto and Vancouver, combined with steady population growth into the southwest quadrant, frames the underwriting case.

Closings are targeted for 2026. Suite selection and premium/non-premium lots are limited, and the Model T cashback promotion is being run on a limited-time basis.

projects.project_name: Glen Abbey Encore – Lifestyle Townhomes An Encore Collection
projects.address: Bronte Rd & Upper Middle Rd W, Oakville, ON

hero.headline: Glen Abbey Encore — Oakville's Ravine Townhomes
hero.subhead: Fernbrook's freehold back-to-back towns and linked detached homes in one of Oakville's last new-build pockets, backing onto 14 Mile Creek.

page_plan.story_md:
Glen Abbey Encore is Fernbrook Homes' final act in Oakville's most established neighbourhood — a freehold collection of back-to-back townhomes and linked detached homes at Bronte Road & Upper Middle Road West, backing onto the Fourteen Mile Creek greenbelt. The Lifestyle Townhomes collection delivers floor plans from 1,626 to 1,918 sq ft (with optional rooftop terraces), while the linked detached residences stretch up to 3,342 sq ft for buyers who need the full single-family footprint.

The location is the story. Glen Abbey Encore is a reflection that this is among the last new home communities that will be built within Glen Abbey, which is the practical reason inventory in this catchment trades at a premium. Golfers will love the proximity to Deerfield Golf Club and many other courses nearby. The beautiful parks, beaches and marinas of Lake Ontario are just minutes south, and commuters will enjoy proximity to Bronte GO Station and Hwys. 407 and the QEW/403. An adjacent commercial plaza brings Canadian Tire, Longos, Walmart and LCBO within a few minutes.

For pre-construction buyers, the deal economics are clean. Back-to-Back Towns require $25,000 with offer plus $15,000 every 90 days to a total of $100,000 — a manageable 10% spread over the build cycle. Development charges are capped at $3,500, the assignment fee is locked at $3,000, and POTL fees come in around $108/month with hydro, gas and water separately metered. Lots with rooftop terraces are priced from $1,059,900, and end-unit Omega plans run $1,089,900.

The Cartier (Model 2101) and Omega (Model 2102) are the two Back-to-Back floor plans on offer, both built on Block 6, 7 and 8 with August–September 2027 completion windows. Stainless steel KitchenAid appliances, granite/quartz counters, hardwood floors and AYA custom cabinetry are included — finishes are not the upgrade-list afterthought they typically are in this price bracket.

selling_points.title: Among the last builds in Glen Abbey
selling_points.body: Glen Abbey Encore is a reflection that this is among the last new home communities that will be built within Glen Abbey — a closed-supply story in a mature, high-demand Oakville catchment.

selling_points.title: Ravine enclave backing 14 Mile Creek
selling_points.body: A ravine enclave community located at Upper Middle & Bronte Road · Just 7 minutes to Bronte Waterfront & backing onto the 14 Mile Creek Greenbelt, with 300+ km of nearby trails.

selling_points.title: Capped levies and low assignment fee
selling_points.body: Development Charges capped at $3,500 and a flat $3,000 Assignment Fee — material protection against the closing-cost surprises that hit most pre-con buyers.

selling_points.body: Stainless Steel KitchenAid package including French Door Bottom-Freezer Fridge, Electric Range, Dishwasher, Hood Fan and Microwave — no upgrade required.

selling_points.body: Commuters will enjoy proximity to Bronte GO Station and Hwys. 407 and the QEW/403. 12 minutes from Oakville Go Station, where you'll have easy access to both GO Train & Go Bus services.

Renowned for its ability to construct unique communities, Fernbrook Homes unquestionably stands at the forefront of the Greater Toronto Area's real estate landscape.

projects.address: Huntmar Dr & Campeau Dr, Kanata, Ottawa, ON

hero.headline: Arcadia Kanata — Minto's Master-Planned Community
hero.subhead: Townhomes and detached homes by Minto at Huntmar & Campeau, steps from Kanata's future LRT station and tech corridor.

page_plan.story_md:
Arcadia is Minto Communities' master-planned neighbourhood at Huntmar Drive and Campeau Drive in central Kanata — a decade-old, established community now expanding with a new chapter of Metro Townhomes, Executive Townhomes, and 36' and 43' Detached Homes. Pricing for Metro Towns starts from $360,327 (excl. HST), with Executive Towns from $574,900 and Detached homes scaling up to just over $1M for the largest 43' Quinton with guest suite.

The location is the story. Arcadia sits directly within Kanata's entertainment and shopping district — minutes from Tanger Outlets, Kanata Centrum, and the Canadian Tire Centre — and a short drive to Kanata North Technology Park, one of Canada's largest tech employment hubs. A future Stage 2 O-Train (Campeau LRT) station is planned within a two-minute walk, and Highway 417 is reachable via Terry Fox Drive. That combination drives the rental demand thesis for investors and the commuter case for end-users.

Minto has tailored this launch to a wide range of buyers. Metro Towns are 2-bedroom stacked towns with separate entrances, private outdoor space, and select underground parking — purpose-built for first-time buyers, with capped $0 development fees, right to lease during occupancy, free assignment (legal admin extra), and a 6-appliance package. Executive Towns and 36'/43' Detached homes scale up to 3-, 4-, and 5-bedroom layouts (including 4+ guest suite configurations) ideal for growing and multi-generational families.

VIP buyers benefit from platinum pricing, Design Centre bonuses up to $10,000, hardwood flooring and quartz countertops, 9' main-floor ceilings, HST rebate savings of up to $130,000, plus free lawyer review and mortgage arrangements. Minto's GOHBA 2025 Production Builder of the Year recognition — its second consecutive win and sixth overall — backs the build quality.

hero.headline: Wild Indigo: Master-Planned Breslau Community
hero.subhead: Madison Group's 1,600-home freehold enclave on the Grand River brings towns and detached homes minutes from Kitchener-Waterloo's tech core.

page_plan.story_md:
Wild Indigo is Madison Group's newest master-planned community located off Fountain Street North in Breslau, and one of the largest residential developments coming to the Kitchener-Waterloo Region. The community delivers a freehold mix of single detached homes and traditional townhomes on the doorstep of the Grand River, with towns priced from $640,699 and 27' detached homes from $720,345 — both after the HST rebate.

The scale is the story here. With a total of 1,600 homes, the community will include single detached homes, freehold townhomes, stacked townhomes, and condominiums. The first phase of development, set to launch in 2025, will introduce 289 carefully crafted homes. Among these are Two-Storey Freehold Traditional Townhomes, ranging in size from 1,408 to 1,662 square feet, each designed with private backyards. Buyers can also size up into 27', 34', or 40' detached lots, giving every household — first-time, move-up, or downsizer — a footprint that fits.

Location underwrites the investment case. It's a 5 minute drive to the future Breslau GO station, 13 minutes to Kitchener GO station, 12 minutes to Highway 8, 15 minutes to Highway 401, and a 90 minute train ride from Kitchener GO to Union Station. Major employers including Google, Vidyard, Shopify, Grand River Hospital, and Toyota Manufacturing all sit within 10-15 minutes, and top universities like the University of Waterloo and Wilfrid Laurier University are a short drive away with quick access to Conestoga Parkway.

The VIP launch package is unusually rich for the Waterloo Region: zero development charges, an 8% deposit, a 6-piece appliance package, and a $125,000 bonus of premium finishes. Combined with up to $130,000 in HST rebate eligibility, the all-in entry point is one of the most aggressive in the GTA-West pre-construction market.

selling_points.title: 1,600-Home Master-Planned Scale
selling_points.body: The community will include single detached homes, freehold townhomes, stacked townhomes, and condominiums, anchoring long-term value through Madison's full build-out.

selling_points.title: Freehold Ownership, No Condo Fees
selling_points.body: Wild Indigo's commitment to providing freehold ownership grants homeowners and investors a greater degree of control over their properties — no condo corporation overhead.

selling_points.title: Zero Development Charges
selling_points.body: Capped development levies and zero DCs save buyers a line-item that typically runs five figures at closing — a rare incentive in the current market.

selling_points.title: $125,000 Premium Finishes Bonus
selling_points.body: Madison's VIP package layers in a 6-piece appliance suite plus $125,000 of bonus finishes — value baked into the purchase, not negotiated at design centre.

selling_points.title: Waterloo Region Tech Backstop
selling_points.body: Major employers such as Google, Vidyard, Shopify, Grand River Hospital, and Toyota Manufacturing sit within 10-15 minutes, supporting a deep end-user and rental pool.

selling_points.title: Two-Way GO Service Coming
selling_points.body: A 5 minute drive to the future Breslau GO station and a 90 minute train ride from Kitchener GO to Union position Wild Indigo as a Toronto commuter play before the infrastructure prices in.

hero.headline: Wasaga Walk: Freehold Towns Steps from the Beach
hero.subhead: A limited collection of 3–5 bedroom freehold townhomes by Primont Homes at 66th Street North & Mosley Street, minutes from Wasaga Beach.

page_plan.story_md:
Wasaga Walk is a limited collection of freehold townhomes by Primont Homes, set at 66th Street North and Mosley Street in the heart of Wasaga Beach. The community sits minutes from the world's longest freshwater beach on Nottawasaga Bay, with 3–5 bedroom layouts designed for families, end-users, and investors who want resort-town living without condo fees.

Pricing starts at $449,990 after the HST rebate — a rare entry point in a market where the developer's published range opens from the $600,000s. Buyers in the VIP release get first access to floor plans and pricing before the public launch, with qualified purchasers eligible to save up to $130,000 through the HST rebate program.

The location is the investment story. Wasaga Beach is one of Ontario's fastest-growing municipalities, with population already up more than 20% and projected to double over the next 30 years. The province has committed a $38-million Destination Wasaga investment to redevelop the waterfront and downtown — adding a boutique hotel, river marina, music hall, and pedestrian bridge — work that is now moving from concept to construction. That public investment, paired with 14 km of shoreline drawing more than two million visitors a year, supports long-term appreciation and short-term rental demand.

Primont Homes has been building since 1971, with more than 5,000 homes delivered across the GTA under second-generation family leadership. For buyers who want pre-construction exposure to a supply-constrained resort market — backed by a builder with a 50-year track record — Wasaga Walk is one of the cleanest entry points on the board.

selling_points.title: Townhomes from $449,990 after HST rebate
selling_points.body: Entry pricing positions Wasaga Walk well below the developer's published from-the-$600,000s range, with the HST rebate saving qualified buyers up to $130,000.

selling_points.title: Freehold tenure — no condo fees
selling_points.body: Primont is delivering a limited collection of freehold townhomes, so owners avoid monthly maintenance fees common to stacked or condo-titled product.

selling_points.title: 3–5 bedroom family layouts
selling_points.body: Floor plans span three to five bedrooms, sized for end-user families as well as multi-bedroom rental demand in a tourism-driven market.

selling_points.title: Steps from the world's longest freshwater beach
selling_points.body: Located at 66th Street North and Mosley Street, the site is walking distance to Nottawasaga Bay's 14 km of sandy shoreline across eight beach areas.

selling_points.title: $38M Destination Wasaga catalyst

projects.address: Powerline Rd & Rest Acres Rd, Paris, ON (Brant County)

hero.headline: Riverbend Towns in Paris from the $400s
hero.subhead: Losani Homes' freehold back-to-back and 3-storey towns inside the Simply Grand master-plan, two minutes from Highway 403.

page_plan.story_md:
Riverbend Townhomes is Losani Homes' newest release inside the established Simply Grand master-planned community in Paris, Ontario, at 1021 Rest Acres Road near Powerline Road. The collection pairs back-to-back towns with full 3-storey designs, ranging from 1,252 to 1,703 sq ft and starting at $437,900 — pricing that's increasingly rare for freehold-style product within an hour of Toronto. Set within the Simply Grand master-planned development, this collection of contemporary townhomes offers a range of thoughtfully crafted floor plans, with prices starting from the $400s, surrounded by scenic trails, schools, and convenient amenities in a peaceful small-town setting.

Location drives the investment thesis. Highway 403 is roughly two minutes away, putting Brantford within 15 minutes and Hamilton inside 30–40. At Riverbend, residents are about an hour to Downtown Toronto and only 30–40 minutes to Hamilton, with easy access to Highway 403. Brantford's VIA Rail and GO Transit station is approximately 15 minutes from the door, opening up a true GTA commute without GTA pricing.

The buyer story is straightforward: end-users get a freehold-feel townhome in one of Ontario's fastest-growing small towns; investors get a low entry point with a 10% deposit spread over 60 days, an assignment clause, capped development charges, and the HST rebate available to qualifying buyers. The deposit structure is $15,000 on signing, 5% minus $15,000 in 60 days, and 5% in 90 days, with incentives including capped development charges at $2,500, $0 assignment (admin fees apply), free lawyer review of the purchase agreement, free mortgage arrangements, and a Losani Home Buyer Guarantee that matches the price if Losani ever lowers it.

The builder is the anchor. With nearly five decades of experience, Losani Homes was founded in 1976 by Giovanni and Lino Losani and later joined by Fred Losani — a family-run builder recognized as one of Canada's Best Managed Companies for nine consecutive years and recipient of over 100 homebuilding awards.

selling_points.title: Freehold-style towns from the $400s
selling_points.body: Riverbend opens at $437,900 for the 1,306 sq ft Bowery interior back-to-back — one of the lowest entry points for a new townhome within an hour of Toronto.

selling_points.body: Direct 403 access puts Brantford 15 minutes away and Hamilton inside 30–40 minutes, with Downtown Toronto roughly an hour out.

selling_points.title: Inside the Simply Grand master-plan
selling_points.body: Riverbend is the newest phase of Losani's established Simply Grand community in Paris, benefiting from existing streetscapes, parks and trail access.

selling_points.title: Investor-friendly terms
selling_points.body: 10% deposit spread over 60 days, capped development charges at $2,500, $0 development levies, and an assignment clause are all on the table.

selling_points.title: Losani Home Buyer Guarantee
selling_points.body: If Losani lowers the price after you sign, they match it — paired with free legal review and free mortgage arrangements.

faq.a: Eligible buyers can save up to $130,000 through the HST new-home rebate. Eligibility depends on whether you (or a qualifying family member) use the home as a primary residence, or on the rental rebate path for investors with a one-year lease.

projects.address: Burke St & Dundas St E, Hamilton, ON

hero.headline: SOL Stacked Towns in Waterdown East
hero.subhead: A 21-unit stacked townhome community at 90 Skinner Road delivering 1- and 2-storey homes minutes from Aldershot GO and Highway 403.

page_plan.story_md:
SOL Stacked Towns is a boutique 21-unit stacked townhome community by New Horizon Development Group, currently in preconstruction at 90 Skinner Road, Hamilton, in the high-growth Waterdown East pocket of Hamilton. The collection pairs 1-storey *Rhythm* flats from 672 sq ft with 2-storey *Radiant* homes up to 1,362 sq ft — a rare entry-price product type in a neighbourhood normally dominated by detached and freehold towns.

The location is the story. The community offers strong access to major routes across Hamilton, Burlington, and the GTA. Residents are close to Highway 403, Highway 407, Highway 5, Highway 6, QEW routes, local bus routes, and Aldershot GO Station. That puts downtown Burlington, McMaster University, and Toronto-bound GO service all inside a short drive — a key fundamental for end-user resale and rental demand.

For investors, the math is unusually clean at this price point: development charges are capped at $0, assignment is free, and pricing already reflects the enhanced HST rebate. Condo fees of just $246/month cover exterior maintenance, landscaping, snow removal, and insurance on the townhome envelopes — meaning carrying costs stay predictable across the hold.

The builder is well-established locally. New Horizon Development Group (NHDG) has established itself as a pioneering force in community development across Southern Ontario since its inception in 1993. With a portfolio that spans over 30 communities, from Oakville to Brantford and beyond, NHDG is renowned for its commitment to meticulous design, superior craftsmanship, and innovative residential solutions. SOL sits next to their completed Interlude and Essence communities in Waterdown — a track record of finished product on the same street grid.

selling_points.title: Entry pricing from $399,990
selling_points.body: 1-storey Rhythm flats start at $399,990 after the enhanced HST rebate — one of the lowest entry points for new-build ownership anywhere in the Waterdown / Burlington corridor.

selling_points.title: Two distinct floorplan formats
selling_points.body: Choose a 672 sq ft 1-bed Rhythm flat or a 1,362 sq ft 3-bed / 2.5-bath Radiant two-storey — covering both investor and end-user demand from a single 21-unit site.

selling_points.title: Investor-friendly terms
selling_points.body: Development charges are capped at $0 and assignment is free, with property management and leasing services available through the builder's preferred providers.

selling_points.title: Highway and GO connectivity
selling_points.body: 5 minutes to Highway 403, 7 minutes to Highway 407, and roughly 10 minutes to Aldershot GO for one-seat service into Union Station.

selling_points.title: Low condo fees, exterior coverage
selling_points.body: $246/month covers maintenance of roads, sidewalks, roofs, exterior windows and doors, landscaping, and snow removal — predictable carrying cost for owners and tenants alike.

selling_points.title: Established Waterdown builder
selling_points.body: New Horizon has been active in Southern Ontario since 1993 with a portfolio spanning over 30 communities, including completed Waterdown projects Interlude, Essence, Parallel, Majestic, and Trend Living.

projects.address: Bronte Rd & Saw Whet Blvd, Oakville, ON

hero.headline: Bronte Riverview: South Oakville at the Park's Edge
hero.subhead: Caivan's freehold towns and detached homes border 350 acres of Bronte Creek Provincial Park, minutes from Bronte GO and the QEW.

urgency_signals: Backs onto Bronte Creek Provincial Park
page_plan.story_md:
Bronte Riverview — also marketed as Riverview South Oakville — is Caivan Communities' pre-construction residential community at the intersection of Bronte Road and Upper Middle Road West, an exclusive enclave of 165 luxury homes. The community sits directly beside 350 acres of Bronte Creek Provincial Park with trails, river valley views, and outdoor recreation, giving every buyer rare daily access to protected greenspace inside one of the GTA's most established municipalities.

The product mix is built for end-users and investors alike. The community includes Riverview Towns, Advantage Townhomes, and 45', 46' & 50' Singles, with townhomes from 1,388 to 2,727 sq ft (3–5 bedrooms, 2.5–4.5 baths) and singles ranging from 2,535 to 3,660 sq ft. The Advantage Townhomes are the first in Oakville to offer a legal 2-storey secondary suite with a private entrance — designed for rental income or multi-generational families, with up to 5 bedrooms and 4.5 bathrooms.

Location is the headline number. It's 1 minute to Highway 403, 5 minutes to Bronte GO Station, 5 minutes to Highway 407, 5 minutes to the Lake Ontario waterfront, 6 minutes to Oakville Trafalgar Memorial Hospital, and 10 minutes to Downtown Oakville. Everyday essentials sit nearby at Longo's, Walmart, Canadian Tire, and LCBO, with access to 23 Michelin Guide restaurants in the surrounding area.

The builder profile reduces typical pre-construction risk. Caivan has a 16-year track record, over 4,200 homes built, and zero cancelled projects, and the VIP launch package layers cash-off incentives, capped closing costs, free assignment, and HST rebate eligibility for investors on top of platinum pricing.

selling_points.title: Directly Beside Bronte Creek Park
selling_points.body: Homes border 350 acres of Bronte Creek Provincial Park with trails, river valley views, and outdoor recreation — a permanently protected backdrop you can't replicate in resale.

selling_points.title: First-in-Oakville Legal Secondary Suites
selling_points.body: The Advantage Townhomes are the first in Oakville to offer a legal 2-storey secondary suite with a private entrance — designed for rental income or multi-generational families, with up to 5 bedrooms and 4.5 bathrooms.

selling_points.title: Five-Minute Transit & Highway Access
selling_points.body: 1 minute to Highway 403, 5 minutes to Bronte GO Station, and 5 minutes to Highway 407 — commuter math that supports long-term resale and rental demand.

selling_points.title: Caivan's Delivery Track Record
selling_points.body: A 16-year track record, over 4,200 homes built, and zero cancelled projects — meaningful protection in a market where cancellations have become common.

selling_points.title: VIP Cash-Off & Capped Costs
selling_points.body: VIP package includes $0 development charge increases, closing costs capped at $5,000, free assignment, up to $120,000 cash off on detached, $80,000 off Advantage Towns, $60,000 off Riverview Towns, and design studio credits up to $20,000.

selling_points.title: Investor-Friendly HST Rebate
selling_points.body: HST rebate of up to $130,000 is available for eligible buyers including investors, materially improving entry pricing versus resale comparables.

faq.a: The VIP package includes an HST rebate of up to $130,000 for eligible buyers, including investors. Eligibility is subject to CRA/government program criteria — not all purchasers will qualify.
